scope.summary:
Build the native Rust/egui LoomSearchV2Surface panel - the UI-only counterpart to the React LoomSearchV2Panel (app/src/components/LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx). The backend loom_search_v2 endpoint (POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/search-v2, implemented in KERNEL-009 MT-264) is already complete; this MT wires the existing API to a new native egui panel.

Diff shape: create src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_search_v2.rs (new flat module, ~350 lines). Register it as a pub mod in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs. Add a LoomSearchV2 pane to the shell's pane registry by reusing the existing pane_registry.rs registration path (the WP-011 pane host - do NOT invent a new panel enum file). Wire the HTTP client calls through the existing backend_client.rs to POST to /workspaces/{id}/loom/search-v2 and to POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/views (save-as-view).

Panel features to implement (parity with React reference):
1. QUERY BAR: single-line egui::TextEdit bound to a query string field. On Enter key or 'Search' button click, fire async HTTP POST to /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/search-v2 with body {query, content_type?, graph_boost: 1.0, limit: 25}. Deserialize LoomSearchV2Response {hits: [{block, score, fts_rank, trgm_sim, vector_sim, edge_degree, highlight}], content_type_facets: BTreeMap<String,i64>, semantic_available: bool, total: i64}.
2. STATUS LINE: show 'Searching...' during load, '{N} results (semantic on/off)' after response, or error text. Use egui::Label with aria-live equivalent.
3. CONTENT-TYPE FACETS: after a response, render one toggle button per entry in content_type_facets (sorted by count descending). Each shows '{type} ({count})'. Clicking a facet toggles it as a filter and immediately re-fires the search with that content_type. Active facet visually highlighted (egui::Button::selected or custom fill).
4. RESULTS LIST: egui::ScrollArea containing one clickable row per LoomSearchV2Hit. Each row shows: block.title (or block_id fallback), block.content_type badge, score formatted to 3 decimal places, and the highlight string. The highlight may contain <mark>...</mark> markers: parse them and render the marked segments in a distinct color (e.g. egui::Color32::YELLOW background on the text run, using egui::RichText or a custom painter). Clicking a row calls the on_open_block callback (routes to the Loom block viewer, wired in the shell via loom_graph.rs).
5. SAVE AS VIEW: a 'Save as view' button, enabled only when there are results. On click, POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views with body {kind: 'table', query: {content_type: <active_facet_or_omit>}, columns: ['title','content_type','updated'], title: 'Search: {query}'}. Show a status label with the new block_id on success or the error string on failure. This reuses the same createBlockView backend path as the MT-262 block-collection view.
6. STATE MACHINE: loading bool, error Option<String>, view_status Option<String>, response Option<LoomSearchV2Response>, active_content_type Option<String>. Keep all state inside LoomSearchV2PanelState struct.

Files to create/edit:
- CREATE: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_search_v2.rs
- EDIT: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs (pub mod loom_search_v2;)
- EDIT: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs (register the LoomSearchV2 pane in the existing WP-011 pane registry)
- EDIT: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs (add loom_search_v2 async fn and create_block_view async fn)

Implementation steps:
1. Define LoomSearchV2PanelState struct with: query: String, active_content_type: Option<String>, response: Option<LoomSearchV2Response>, loading: bool, error: Option<String>, view_status: Option<String>.
2. Define LoomSearchV2Response, LoomSearchV2Hit, LoomBlock as serde-deserializable structs in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs (matching the backend JSON schema from storage/loom.rs).
3. Implement async fn loom_search_v2(base_url, workspace_id, body) -> Result<LoomSearchV2Response, String> using the existing backend_client.rs HTTP client. POST to {base_url}/workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/search-v2.
4. Implement async fn create_block_view(base_url, workspace_id, body) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String>. POST to {base_url}/workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views.
5. In the panel show() fn: render query TextEdit + Search button + Save-as-view button in a horizontal strip.
6. Render status line.
7. Render facet buttons if response.content_type_facets non-empty (sort by value desc).
8. Render egui::ScrollArea with result rows. For each hit parse highlight: split on '<mark>' and '</mark>' tokens, build a LayoutJob with alternating normal/highlighted TextFormat runs.
9. Handle AccessKit (reuse the WP-011 accessibility/ subsystem - accessibility/mod.rs, accessibility/registry.rs, accessibility/snapshot.rs): assign author_ids to query input (id: 'loom-search-v2.query'), search button ('loom-search-v2.search'), save-view button ('loom-search-v2.save-view'), each facet button ('loom-search-v2.facet.{content_type}'), each result row ('loom-search-v2.result.{block_id}'), status label ('loom-search-v2.status'). Use the egui AccessKit integration via accessibility/registry.rs (egui::Id from stable strings) and set role + action where egui_kittest requires.
10. Async dispatch: egui is single-threaded; use the shell's existing async bridge (tokio one-shot or channel pattern used by other WP-011 surfaces, e.g. event_bus.rs) to fire the search and receive the response on the next frame. Do not block the UI thread.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: requires the WP-011 shell pane infrastructure to be loadable (the pane_registry.rs registry must be able to host this pane). The backend /loom/search-v2 endpoint is already live (KERNEL-009 MT-264). No new backend work needed. This MT is self-contained and has no MT-level depends_on within WP-012.

Risk if missed: Loom Search v2 is a core search surface and a dependency for the interconnection proofs (MT-030, MT-044). Missing it means the quick-switcher and parity proof cannot demonstrate the semantic search modality.
acceptance_criteria: LoomSearchV2PanelState struct exists in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_search_v2.rs with fields: query, active_content_type, response (Option<LoomSearchV2Response>), loading, error, view_status.
Typing a query and pressing Enter (or the Search button) fires a real HTTP POST to /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/search-v2 against a running handshake_core instance and populates the response.
Status line shows '{N} results (semantic on)' or '{N} results (keyword/fuzzy only)' based on semantic_available flag.
Content-type facet buttons appear after a result with multiple content types; clicking a facet re-runs the search with that content_type filter; clicking the active facet again clears it.
Each result row renders: block.title (or block_id fallback), content_type badge, score to 3 decimal places, highlight with <mark> segments rendered in a distinct color (not as raw HTML tags).
Clicking a result row invokes the on_open_block callback with the correct block_id.
Save as view button is disabled with no results and enabled with results; clicking it POSTs to /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views and displays the returned block_id in the view_status label.
All interactive widgets have stable AccessKit author_ids matching the naming contract in implementation_notes.
The panel is registered in the WP-011 pane registry (pane_registry.rs) and can be opened/closed without crashing.
cargo build -p handshake-native succeeds with no new warnings attributable to this MT.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native loom_search_v2 -- --nocapture: a real-PG integration test (src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_loom_search_v2.rs) that spawns handshake_core, creates a workspace, seeds at least 3 LoomBlocks (different content_types), calls POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/search-v2 with a query matching the seeded blocks, and asserts: hits.len() >= 1, each hit has block.block_id, score > 0.0, highlight is a non-empty string, content_type_facets has entries. (Test uses the same fixture pattern as the existing workspace-search fixture.)

cargo test -p handshake-native loom_search_v2_save_view -- --nocapture: seeds blocks, runs search, calls POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/views with the save-as-view body, asserts HTTP 200 and response contains block.block_id.

egui_kittest snapshot test: render LoomSearchV2Panel with a mock response (inject 3 hits, 2 facets), assert AccessKit tree contains nodes with author_ids 'loom-search-v2.query', 'loom-search-v2.search', 'loom-search-v2.save-view', 'loom-search-v2.facet.note', 'loom-search-v2.result.{block_id_0}'.

Screenshot proof: a screenshot taken via egui_kittest or a headless render showing the panel with the query bar, at least 2 facet buttons, and 3 result rows with visible highlight coloring (mark segments visible).
implementation_notes: Highlight parsing: split the highlight string on the literal tokens '<mark>' and '</mark>'. Build an egui::text::LayoutJob alternating between TextFormat::default() and a TextFormat with background: Color32::from_rgb(255, 214, 0) for marked segments. Do not use dangerouslySetInnerHTML or raw HTML; egui renders layout jobs natively.

Async pattern: use tokio::sync::oneshot or a std::sync::mpsc channel stored in the panel state (the same async bridge pattern WP-011 surfaces use via event_bus.rs). On search trigger, spawn a tokio task (tokio::spawn) that makes the HTTP call and sends the result back through the channel. On each frame, poll the receiver (try_recv) and update state if a message arrived. This is the standard egui async pattern.
HTTP client: reuse the existing reqwest::Client owned by backend_client.rs; do not construct a new client. Add the loom_search_v2 and create_block_view async fns to backend_client.rs.
Facet sort: iterate response.content_type_facets (BTreeMap<String,i64>) and collect into a Vec, sort by value descending, then count ascending for ties.

Save-as-view body: POST {kind: 'table', query: active_content_type.map(|ct| json!({content_type: ct})).unwrap_or(json!({})), columns: ['title','content_type','updated']} with a block_patch {title: format!('Search: {}', query.trim())} as per the React reference (LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx lines 111-121). Confirm the /loom/views endpoint signature from backend_client.rs before coding.

AccessKit IDs: register via the WP-011 accessibility/registry.rs subsystem using egui::Id::new('loom-search-v2.query') etc. For result rows use egui::Id::new(format!('loom-search-v2.result.{}', hit.block.block_id)). Register these with egui's AccessKit writer (accessibility/snapshot.rs) so egui_kittest can assert them.

Pane registration: register the LoomSearchV2 pane through the existing WP-011 pane_registry.rs registry (do NOT add a new panel-enum file). In the pane dispatch path, call loom_search_v2::show(ui, state, callbacks). The pane is typically opened from the search rail (search_rail.rs) or a top menu item (top_menu_bar.rs).

LoomBlock struct in Rust (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs): { block_id: String, workspace_id: String, content_type: String, title: Option<String>, ... } matching the backend storage/loom.rs LoomBlock. Only deserialize the fields needed for display.
graph_boost default: always send 1.0 as the React reference does (LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx line 89). limit: 25.

Cross-WP dependency (prose, not a depends_on entry): this MT requires the WP-011 shell pane infrastructure (pane_registry.rs), the accessibility/ subsystem, backend_client.rs, and event_bus.rs to be present and loadable. These are delivered by WP-KERNEL-011 and are assumed available in the source tree; there is no WP-012 MT-level depends_on for this MT.

THEME-GUARD ON THE <mark> HIGHLIGHT (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23 - PROACTIVE, just fixed this exact class for the tag palette): impl note 67 says background Color32::from_rgb(255,214,0) for marked segments - that opaque `Color32::from_rgb(` form TRIPS the CONTROL-4 guard test_theme::no_hardcoded_color32_outside_theme_module (it exempts ONLY palette.rs/syntax.rs). Do NOT put a from_rgb literal in loom_search_v2.rs. Instead either (a) ADD a search_highlight_bg token to theme/palette.rs (added to allowed_paths) and read it via the palette, or (b) reuse an existing warning/accent palette token, or (c) use Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(...) (the sanctioned translucent-affordance form the guard does NOT flag). The correctness lens runs the BARE cargo test (incl test_theme) - a from_rgb literal will fail the gate.

BACKEND PROOF REALITY + ROUTE VERIFY (MT-008/021-027 pattern, MANDATORY): the search IS the deliverable - it needs the REAL POST /loom/search-v2 (the contract cites api/loom.rs:2679 + storage/loom.rs:637-696 - VERIFY those + the LoomSearchV2Response{hits,content_type_facets,semantic_available,total} + LoomSearchV2Hit{block,score,fts_rank,trgm_sim,vector_sim,edge_degree,highlight} field shape read-only). SAVE-AS-VIEW: align with MT-027's VERIFIED createBlockView route - MT-027 found block-views live at POST /loom/views/definitions (NOT the contract's bare /loom/views); verify + use the same route MT-027 bound (RISK-3/MC-3: do NOT guess). Bind real; gate the live-search ACs behind the integration feature + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF if no seeded backend. The <mark> highlight LayoutJob parser ('<mark>foo</mark> bar <mark>baz</mark>' -> 3 segments, mid+last marked, MC-1), facet sort (value desc), facet-clear-on-query (MC-4), no-workspace guard (MC-7), and semantic_available true/false status are unit/kittest-testable STANDALONE with a mock response.

ASYNC + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): the HTTP search MUST be off the UI thread (tokio::spawn + channel, poll try_recv per frame - reuse the existing backend_client.rs reqwest::Client + the WP-011 async-bridge pattern); a synchronous call freezes egui (RISK-2, HBR-QUIET). NO PERPETUAL SPINNER - the 'Searching...' state animates ONLY while a genuine request is in flight; idle/headless neutral (MT-015 lesson); kittest uses step() not run(). serde structs MUST match the snake_case backend JSON keys exactly (RISK-6 - a mismatch silently nulls fields). Flat module placement (src/loom_search_v2.rs + lib.rs pub mod + pane_registry.rs registration) is CORRECT for a pane (not a graph/ sub-widget). Screenshots to the EXTERNAL Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-012-mt-028/ root + assert_no_local_artifact_dir(). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: field-correct accesskit 0.21.1 roles; ACs assert the loom-search-v2.* author_ids.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx (primary reference - full feature set; ported to src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_search_v2.rs)
app/src/lib/loom_search_query.ts (operator parser - implement equivalent Rust fn if needed for future operator syntax; not required for MT-028 baseline)
app/src/lib/loom_search_open_target.ts (open target resolution - the on_open_block callback in this MT covers the loom_block case; full target dispatch is MT-030)
binds_backend_api: POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/search-v2 - body: LoomSearchV2Body {query, content_type?, tag_ids?, graph_boost, limit, offset?} - response: LoomSearchV2Response {hits, content_type_facets, semantic_available, total} (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs:2679, storage/loom.rs:637-696)

POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views - body: {kind, query, columns} + block patch {title} - used for save-as-view (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs, list routes section for /loom/views)

risks: Highlight rendering: if <mark> parsing is skipped or deferred, the panel will show raw HTML tags instead of colored text, breaking the fidelity proof.
Async blocking: if the HTTP call is made synchronously on the UI thread, egui will freeze during search, violating HBR-QUIET.
Save-as-view endpoint path: the /loom/views route may differ from the React createBlockView path; if the model guesses the wrong route, the feature will fail silently.
Facet toggle state desync: if active_content_type is not cleared when the query changes, stale facet filters will silently narrow search results.
AccessKit IDs missing: if author_ids are not registered, swarm agents cannot drive the panel and the HBR-SWARM proof fails.
LoomSearchV2Response struct mismatch: if the Rust struct's field names don't match the snake_case JSON keys from the backend, serde will silently produce empty/null fields.
semantic_available false path: if the panel doesn't handle the false case, it may show '(semantic on)' even when the backend is in keyword-only mode, misleading the operator.
No workspace selected: if workspace_id is None and the search is triggered, the backend will 404; the panel must guard and show an error rather than crashing.
minimum_controls: Highlight parser must be tested: at minimum a unit test asserting that '<mark>foo</mark> bar <mark>baz</mark>' produces 3 segments with the middle and last marked.
Async proof: the integration test must not block; confirm the UI loop remains responsive during the HTTP call (egui_kittest frame advance with pending future).
Save-as-view route: verify the exact route by reading backend_client.rs before coding; do not guess.
Facet clear on new query: state machine must reset active_content_type = None whenever query changes.
All 6 AccessKit node IDs listed in implementation_notes must appear in the egui_kittest snapshot test.
Test the semantic_available=false path: seed a workspace without pgvector and assert the status line shows '(keyword/fuzzy only)'.
Guard no-workspace case: if workspace_id is None, show error label 'No workspace selected' and return early without making an HTTP call.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + the just-fixed tag-palette theme-guard regression + MT-027 createBlockView route + MT-008 backend pattern + MT-015 idle-repaint
corrections: <mark> highlight bg must NOT use Color32::from_rgb( (theme guard) - theme token / from_rgba_unmultiplied; theme/palette.rs in allowed_paths
verify search + save-as-view routes (align save-as-view with MT-027's /loom/views/definitions, don't guess)
off-thread async (no UI freeze) + no perpetual spinner; serde snake_case match; flat pane module; external screenshots; accesskit variant

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_04384b64 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: c0b5ea32 feat MT-028 native LoomSearchV2 surface (E4 search)
cc346c71 fix harden per adversarial review
implementation_result.review_panel: correctness=PASS(0), backend-shape=PASS(0), perf-focus-hygiene=CHANGES_REQUIRED(1) -> 1 must-fix hardened (the perf lens caught it this MT - all 3 lenses contributing across MTs).
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1/3/4/5/6/7/8/9/10 PASS; AC-2 + PT-1/PT-2 live-search = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF (gated, real backend health was empty); 965 lib + 11 unit + 13 integration + test_theme 19/19; clippy clean; search panel (query/Search/Save-as-view + '3 results (keyword/fuzzy only)' + note(2)/code(1) facets + 3 rows with AMBER <mark> highlights + content-type badges + scores) visually verified; screenshot EXTERNAL (git ls-files src/**/*.png empty).
implementation_result.proactive_theme_catch_held: My PROACTIVE theme-guard patch WORKED: the agent added a search_highlight_bg token (amber rgb(255,214,0)) to the CONTROL-4-exempt theme/palette.rs (HsPalette + light/dark + overrides) and loom_search_v2.rs reads it - NO Color32 literal in the feature module; test_theme::no_hardcoded_color32 PASSES. Prevented a repeat of the MT-023 tag-palette regression.
implementation_result.route_alignment_held: Save-as-view reuses MT-027's VERIFIED post_create_block_view -> POST /loom/views/definitions (NOT the contract's guessed /loom/views). Search binds the real POST /loom/search-v2 (verified api/loom.rs).
implementation_result.pane_mounted: Registered as PaneType::LoomSearchV2 in pane_registry.rs + app.rs build_default_factories (HOSTED/openable/closable) + module_switcher tab-id - the FIRST WP-012 surface actually mounted in the running shell (app.rs). 47 pane/switcher/layout regression tests green - no breakage from the new variant.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: backend_client.rs (LoomSearchV2Client) + theme/palette.rs (highlight token) + lib.rs/pane_registry.rs/app.rs/module_switcher.rs (pane registration) + tests/test_loom_search_v2.rs - all in/added to allowed_paths; pane registration necessarily touches the shell registration files.
